tenors.narrative: Prediction markets see WTI trading near current levels by end-April 2026, with a median forecast of $96/bbl (-0.9% from spot). The 25th-75th percentile range of $85-$107 suggests moderate conviction around flat prices, though tail risks extend toward $79 (10th percentile) and $114 (90th percentile).

By end-June 2026, prediction markets show significantly more downside bias with a median of $77/bbl (-20.3% from spot), implying a material decline from current levels. The wide 25th-75th range of $67-$133 and substantial tail risk to $165 reflect high uncertainty, though the median clearly anchors below the April forecast.

The market shows meaningful dispersion, with the interquartile range spanning $4,200-$4,642, while the 10th-90th percentile range widens to $4,200-$5,615, reflecting substantial uncertainty about macro conditions over the next 18 months.
